v_plants — Paired Resurvey Analysis

Valtýr

2026-03-09

library(tidyverse)
library(vegan)
library(broom)
library(ggrepel)
library(indicspecies)
library(knitr)

set.seed(123)
# Knit runs in a clean session. Use shared prep script as single source of truth.
prep_script <- "v_plants_prep.R"
if (!file.exists(prep_script)) {
  stop("Required script not found: ", prep_script)
}
source(prep_script, local = .GlobalEnv)

Purpose. This document picks up directly after v_plants_variable_screening.html. All core objects are created by v_plants_prep.R (sourced above); the screening document is an audit trail that describes how they were selected and filtered.

Object Description
community_long_paired Long-format cover table (plot × sample × taxon)
community_wide_strict Wide community matrix with plot_number + sample columns
meta_strict Metadata aligned row-for-row with community_wide_strict
mat_strict Numeric species matrix (320 rows × 226 cols)
nmds_strict metaMDS result on mat_strict (all plots, pre-outlier removal)
fit_strict envfit result on nmds_strict
vars_driver_strict_final, driver_final Variable vectors from screening

Part 1 — Preliminary NMDS (all plots)

1.1 Ordination plot

This first pass runs on the full 160-plot dataset before any outlier removal. It is included here as a diagnostic to show why the three coastal/dune plots need special treatment.

```{r nmds-all-plot, fig.height=7, fig.cap=sprintf(“NMDS of all %d revisited plots. Note the three outlier sites near NMDS1 = 17 that stretch the axis and compress the main community cloud.”, length(unique(meta_strict$plot_number)))}

Extract site scores

nmds_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(nmds_strict, display = “sites”)) %>% bind_cols(meta_strict %>% select(plot_number, sample))

— envfit arrows (continuous vectors) —

sig_thresh <- 0.05

vec_df <- tibble(variable = character(), NMDS1 = numeric(), NMDS2 = numeric(), r2 = numeric(), p_value = numeric(), kind = character())

if (!is.null(fit_strict\(vectors) && !is.null(fit_strict\)vectors\(arrows)) { vec_raw <- as.data.frame(fit_strict\)vectors\(arrows) %>% rownames_to_column("variable") vec_axes <- setdiff(names(vec_raw), "variable") if (length(vec_axes) >= 2) { vec_df <- vec_raw %>% rename(NMDS1 = all_of(vec_axes[1]), NMDS2 = all_of(vec_axes[2])) %>% select(variable, NMDS1, NMDS2) %>% mutate( r2 = as.numeric(fit_strict\)vectors\(r[variable]), p_value = as.numeric(fit_strict\)vectors$pvals[variable]), kind = “numeric” ) } }

— envfit factor centroids —

fac_df <- tibble(variable = character(), NMDS1 = numeric(), NMDS2 = numeric(), r2 = numeric(), p_value = numeric(), kind = character())

if (!is.null(fit_strict\(factors) && !is.null(fit_strict\)factors\(centroids)) { fac_raw <- as.data.frame(fit_strict\)factors$centroids) %>% rownames_to_column(“variable”) fac_axes <- setdiff(names(fac_raw), “variable”) if (length(fac_axes) >= 2) { fac_df <- fac_raw %>% rename(NMDS1 = all_of(fac_axes[1]), NMDS2 = all_of(fac_axes[2])) %>% select(variable, NMDS1, NMDS2) %>% mutate(r2 = NA_real_, p_value = NA_real_, kind = “factor”) } }

arrow_df_all <- bind_rows(vec_df, fac_df) %>% filter(is.na(p_value) | p_value <= sig_thresh)

ordi_radius_all <- max(abs(c(nmds_scores\(NMDS1, nmds_scores\)NMDS2))) * 0.7

arrow_df_all <- arrow_df_all %>% mutate(xend = NMDS1 * ordi_radius_all, yend = NMDS2 * ordi_radius_all)

ggplot(nmds_scores, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) + geom_line(aes(group = plot_number), colour = “grey70”, linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) + geom_point(aes(colour = sample, shape = sample), size = 2.0, alpha = 0.85) + geom_segment( data = arrow_df_all %>% filter(kind == “numeric”), aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = xend, yend = yend), arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, “cm”)), colour = “#c0392b”, linewidth = 0.7, inherit.aes = FALSE ) + geom_label_repel( data = arrow_df_all %>% filter(kind == “numeric”), aes(x = xend * 1.1, y = yend * 1.1, label = variable), size = 2.8, colour = “#c0392b”, fill = alpha(“white”, 0.7), label.size = 0, max.overlaps = 20, seed = 42, inherit.aes = FALSE ) + scale_colour_manual(values = c(first = “#2980b9”, last = “#e67e22”), labels = c(first = “First survey”, last = “Last survey”)) + scale_shape_manual(values = c(first = 16, last = 17), labels = c(first = “First survey”, last = “Last survey”)) + labs( title = “NMDS of plant community composition (Bray–Curtis) — all plots”, subtitle = sprintf(“%d plots · stress = %.3f · paired PERMANOVA R² = 2.7%%, p = 0.001”, length(unique(nmds_scores\(plot_number)), nmds_strict\)stress), x = “NMDS1”, y = “NMDS2”, colour = NULL, shape = NULL, caption = “Arrows: envfit vectors (p ≤ 0.05). Lines connect first and last survey of the same plot.” ) + theme_classic(base_size = 11) + theme(legend.position = “bottom”)


## 1.2 Outlier identification

The three plots sitting far to the right are coastal/sandy communities.
Their species composition is so distinct from the rest of the Icelandic
vegetation dataset that NMDS places them ~17 units away from the main cloud,
stretching the axis and making the central cloud unreadable.


``` r
# Identify the six rows (first + last) for the three outlier plots
outlier_plots <- c("RN-02", "RN-03", "RN-65-01")

meta_outliers <- meta_strict %>%
  filter(plot_number %in% outlier_plots) %>%
  select(
    plot_number, sample,
    háplöntuþekja, soil_depth, total_cover,
    any_of(c("vegetation_height_mean", "soil_type_name")),
    moisture_name, topography_name
  )

print_full(meta_outliers,
           caption = "Metadata for the three outlier plots (both surveys).")
Metadata for the three outlier plots (both surveys).
plot_number sample háplöntuþekja soil_depth total_cover vegetation_height_mean moisture_name topography_name
RN-02 first 9.0857 115 5.1429 2.4643 Þurrlendi Jafnlendi
RN-02 last 3.0000 110 1.5000 0.0000 Þurrlendi Jafnlendi
RN-03 first 25.7667 115 25.1333 1.3333 Þurrlendi Jafnlendi
RN-03 last 55.3333 110 52.0000 27.5500 Þurrlendi Jafnlendi
RN-65-01 first 83.0000 90 94.3500 4.7625 Mýri Jafnlendi
RN-65-01 last 67.1667 115 66.6667 3.0000 Flói Jafnlendi 
# Which taxa are responsible for the outlier separation?
# Compare mean relative abundance in outlier vs non-outlier rows.
outlier_rows     <- meta_strict$plot_number %in% outlier_plots
mat_rel          <- mat_strict / rowSums(mat_strict)

taxa_shift <- tibble(
  taxon               = colnames(mat_strict),
  mean_rel_outlier    = colMeans(mat_rel[outlier_rows,  , drop = FALSE]),
  mean_rel_non_outlier = colMeans(mat_rel[!outlier_rows, , drop = FALSE])
) %>%
  mutate(delta_rel = mean_rel_outlier - mean_rel_non_outlier) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(delta_rel)))

print_full(taxa_shift %>% slice_head(n = 30),
           caption = "Top 30 taxa driving the outlier separation (|Δ relative abundance|).")
Top 30 taxa driving the outlier separation (|Δ relative abundance|).
taxon mean_rel_outlier mean_rel_non_outlier delta_rel
Leymus arenarius 0.2485 0.0017 0.2468
Puccinellia coarctata 0.2600 0.0132 0.2468
Honckenya peploides 0.1555 0.0000 0.1555
Mertensia maritima 0.1399 0.0000 0.1399
Cakile maritima 0.1227 0.0000 0.1227
Empetrum nigrum 0.0000 0.0663 -0.0663
Plantago maritima 0.0708 0.0079 0.0629
Carex nigra 0.0000 0.0621 -0.0621
Carex lyngbyei 0.0000 0.0488 -0.0488
Festuca rubra 0.0000 0.0442 -0.0442
Salix arctica 0.0000 0.0428 -0.0428
Vaccinium uliginosum 0.0000 0.0377 -0.0377
Eriophorum angustifolium 0.0000 0.0367 -0.0367
Racomitrium lanuginosum 0.0000 0.0343 -0.0343
Agrostis stolonifera 0.0000 0.0310 -0.0310
Carex bigelowii 0.0000 0.0306 -0.0306
Salix herbacea 0.0000 0.0293 -0.0293
Carex rariflora 0.0000 0.0291 -0.0291
Betula nana 0.0000 0.0280 -0.0280
Carex rostrata 0.0000 0.0254 -0.0254
Bistorta vivipara 0.0000 0.0202 -0.0202
Calamagrostis neglecta 0.0000 0.0194 -0.0194
Silene acaulis 0.0000 0.0144 -0.0144
Armeria maritima 0.0005 0.0137 -0.0133
Festuca vivipara 0.0000 0.0120 -0.0120
Juncus arcticus 0.0000 0.0107 -0.0107
Equisetum variegatum 0.0000 0.0099 -0.0099

Part 2 — Core NMDS (outliers excluded)

keep_rows <- !meta_strict$plot_number %in% outlier_plots
meta_core <- meta_strict[keep_rows, ]
mat_core  <- mat_strict[keep_rows, ]

# Drop taxa that only occurred in the removed plots
taxa_present <- colSums(mat_core) > 0
mat_core     <- mat_core[, taxa_present]

After removing 3 plots (RN-02, RN-03, RN-65-01), the core matrix has 314 rows and 223 taxa. The three excluded plots are analysed separately in Part 5.

2.1 Re-run NMDS

nmds_core <- metaMDS(
  mat_core,
  distance = "bray",
  k        = 2,
  trymax   = 200,
  trace    = FALSE
)

NMDS stress (core, k = 2): 0.2025

Stress interpretation: Poor — consider k = 3 or interpret with caution.

⚠️ Stress > 0.20. Consider re-running with k = 3. A 3D ordination can be visualised as a pairs plot (pairs(scores(nmds_core))).

Shepard plot

stressplot(nmds_core,
           main = sprintf("Shepard plot — stress %.4f", nmds_core$stress))

Shepard diagram: ordination distances vs. dissimilarities. Points should follow the step function tightly.

2.2 Re-fit environmental vectors

env_core <- meta_core %>%
  select(all_of(vars_driver_strict_final)) %>%
  mutate(
    across(where(is.character), as.factor),
    across(where(is.logical),   as.factor)
  )

fit_core <- envfit(nmds_core, env_core, permutations = 999)
fit_tbl_core <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    variable = names(fit_core$vectors$r),
    r2       = as.numeric(fit_core$vectors$r),
    p_value  = as.numeric(fit_core$vectors$pvals),
    kind     = "numeric"
  ),
  if (!is.null(fit_core$factors)) tibble(
    variable = names(fit_core$factors$r),
    r2       = as.numeric(fit_core$factors$r),
    p_value  = as.numeric(fit_core$factors$pvals),
    kind     = "factor"
  )
) %>%
  arrange(p_value) %>%
  mutate(sig = case_when(p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
                         p_value < 0.01  ~ "**",
                         p_value < 0.05  ~ "*",
                         TRUE            ~ "ns"))

print_full(fit_tbl_core,
           caption = "envfit results on core dataset (outliers removed). r² = goodness-of-fit.")
envfit results on core dataset (outliers removed). r² = goodness-of-fit.
variable r2 p_value kind sig
háplöntuþekja 0.4971 0.001 numeric **
soil_depth 0.3389 0.001 numeric **
total_cover 0.4113 0.001 numeric **
vegetation_height_mean 0.4618 0.001 numeric **
moisture_name 0.2723 0.001 factor **
topography_name 0.0205 0.064 factor ns
permafrost 0.0054 0.176 factor ns

2.3 NMDS biplot

```{r nmds-core-plot, fig.height=7.5, fig.cap=sprintf(“NMDS biplot of the core %d-plot dataset. Red arrows are significant envfit vectors (p ≤ 0.05); length is proportional to r². Grey lines connect first and last survey of the same plot.”, length(unique(meta_core$plot_number)))}

— Build arrow data frame —

sig_thresh <- 0.05

arrow_df_core <- as.data.frame(fit_core\(vectors\)arrows) %>% rownames_to_column(“variable”) %>% rename(ax1 = NMDS1, ax2 = NMDS2) %>% mutate( r2 = fit_core\(vectors\)r, p_value = fit_core\(vectors\)pvals ) %>% filter(p_value <= sig_thresh)

Factor centroids

factor_df_core <- tryCatch({ as.data.frame(scores(fit_core, display = “factors”)) %>% rownames_to_column(“label”) %>% rename(fax1 = NMDS1, fax2 = NMDS2) %>% mutate( p_value = fit_core\(factors\)pvals[ sapply(label, function(nm) { matched <- names(fit_core\(factors\)pvals)[startsWith(nm, names(fit_core\(factors\)pvals))] if (length(matched)) matched[1] else NA_character_ }) ] ) %>% filter(!is.na(p_value), p_value <= sig_thresh) }, error = function(e) tibble())

— Site scores —

site_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(nmds_core, display = “sites”)) %>% bind_cols(meta_core %>% select(plot_number, sample))

ordi_radius_core <- max(abs(c(site_scores\(NMDS1, site_scores\)NMDS2))) * 0.70

arrow_df_core <- arrow_df_core %>% mutate(xend = ax1 * ordi_radius_core, yend = ax2 * ordi_radius_core)

— Plot —

p_nmds_core <- ggplot(site_scores, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) + geom_line(aes(group = plot_number), colour = “grey60”, linewidth = 0.30, alpha = 0.55) + geom_point(aes(colour = sample, shape = sample), size = 2.0, alpha = 0.85) + geom_segment( data = arrow_df_core, aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = xend, yend = yend), arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.22, “cm”), type = “closed”), colour = “#c0392b”, linewidth = 0.75, inherit.aes = FALSE ) + geom_label_repel( data = arrow_df_core, aes(x = xend * 1.12, y = yend * 1.12, label = variable), size = 3.0, colour = “#c0392b”, fill = alpha(“white”, 0.80), label.size = 0, max.overlaps = 20, seed = 42, inherit.aes = FALSE ) + { if (nrow(factor_df_core) > 0) geom_label_repel( data = factor_df_core, aes(x = fax1, y = fax2, label = label), size = 2.5, colour = “#27ae60”, fill = alpha(“white”, 0.80), label.size = 0, max.overlaps = 15, seed = 43, inherit.aes = FALSE ) } + scale_colour_manual(values = c(first = “#2980b9”, last = “#e67e22”), labels = c(first = “First survey”, last = “Last survey”)) + scale_shape_manual(values = c(first = 16, last = 17), labels = c(first = “First survey”, last = “Last survey”)) + coord_equal() + labs( title = “NMDS — core dataset (Bray–Curtis)”, subtitle = sprintf( “%d plots · stress = %.3f · excl. %s”, length(unique(site_scores\(plot_number)), nmds_core\)stress, paste(outlier_plots, collapse = “,”) ), x = “NMDS1”, y = “NMDS2”, colour = NULL, shape = NULL, caption = “Red arrows: envfit vectors (p ≤ 0.05); length ∝ r².lines connect first and last survey of the same plot.” ) + theme_classic(base_size = 11) + theme( legend.position = “bottom”, plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8, colour = “grey40”), plot.caption = element_text(size = 7.5, colour = “grey50”, hjust = 0) )

p_nmds_core


---

# Part 3 — Per-plot compositional turnover

## 3.1 Bray–Curtis distance per plot (first → last)


``` r
bc_matrix <- as.matrix(vegdist(mat_strict, method = "bray"))

plot_bc <- meta_strict %>%
  mutate(row_idx = row_number()) %>%
  group_by(plot_number) %>%
  summarise(
    idx_first = row_idx[sample == "first"],
    idx_last  = row_idx[sample == "last"],
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(bc_dist = map2_dbl(idx_first, idx_last, ~ bc_matrix[.x, .y]))

env_first <- meta_strict %>%
  filter(sample == "first") %>%
  select(plot_number, any_of("soil_type_name"), moisture_name,
         háplöntuþekja, soil_depth, any_of("vegetation_height_mean"), total_cover)

plot_bc_env <- plot_bc %>%
  left_join(env_first, by = "plot_number")
bc_summary <- plot_bc_env %>%
  group_by(across(any_of("soil_type_name"))) %>%
  summarise(
    n         = n(),
    median_bc = round(median(bc_dist, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    mean_bc   = round(mean(bc_dist,   na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd_bc     = round(sd(bc_dist,     na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(median_bc))

print_full(bc_summary,
           caption = "Per-plot Bray–Curtis distance (first → last) summarised by soil type.")
Per-plot Bray–Curtis distance (first → last) summarised by soil type.
n median_bc mean_bc sd_bc
160 0.611 0.585 0.211

3.2 Kruskal–Wallis tests

if ("soil_type_name" %in% names(plot_bc_env)) {
  kw_soil <- kruskal.test(bc_dist ~ soil_type_name, data = plot_bc_env)
} else {
  kw_soil <- NULL
}

kw_moist <- kruskal.test(bc_dist ~ moisture_name,  data = plot_bc_env)

kw_tbl <- tibble(
  test      = c("Soil type", "Moisture class"),
  chi_sq    = c(if (!is.null(kw_soil)) kw_soil$statistic  else NA_real_,
                kw_moist$statistic),
  df        = c(if (!is.null(kw_soil)) kw_soil$parameter  else NA_real_,
                kw_moist$parameter),
  p_value   = c(if (!is.null(kw_soil)) kw_soil$p.value    else NA_real_,
                kw_moist$p.value),
  sig       = case_when(p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
                        p_value < 0.01  ~ "**",
                        p_value < 0.05  ~ "*",
                        TRUE            ~ "ns")
)

print_full(kw_tbl,
           caption = "Kruskal–Wallis: does soil type / moisture predict magnitude of compositional change?")
Kruskal–Wallis: does soil type / moisture predict magnitude of compositional change?
test chi_sq df p_value sig
Soil type NA NA NA ns
Moisture class 12.2489 3 0.0066 ** 
if (!is.null(kw_soil) && kw_soil$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("> **Soil type is significant (p < 0.05).** Consider a post-hoc Dunn test:\n")
  cat("> `dunn.test::dunn.test(plot_bc_env$bc_dist, plot_bc_env$soil_type_name, method = 'bh')`\n\n")
}
if (kw_moist$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("> **Moisture class is significant (p < 0.05).** Consider a post-hoc Dunn test:\n")
  cat("> `dunn.test::dunn.test(plot_bc_env$bc_dist, plot_bc_env$moisture_name, method = 'bh')`\n\n")
}

Moisture class is significant (p < 0.05). Consider a post-hoc Dunn test: dunn.test::dunn.test(plot_bc_env$bc_dist, plot_bc_env$moisture_name, method = 'bh')

3.3 Boxplot by soil type

if ("soil_type_name" %in% names(plot_bc_env)) {
  ggplot(
    plot_bc_env %>% filter(!is.na(soil_type_name)),
    aes(x = reorder(soil_type_name, bc_dist, FUN = median),
        y = bc_dist, fill = soil_type_name)
  ) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 1.2) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.4, size = 1) +
    labs(
      title    = "Compositional turnover by soil type",
      subtitle = "Per-plot Bray–Curtis distance (first → last survey)",
      x = "Soil type", y = "Bray–Curtis distance", fill = NULL
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "none")
} else {
  ggplot() +
    theme_void() +
    ggtitle("Soil type not available in meta_strict / plot_bc_env")
}

Per-plot Bray–Curtis distance (first → last survey) by soil type. Points are individual plots.

Part 4 — Indicator species analysis (IndVal)

Using indicspecies::multipatt() to identify taxa that are significantly associated with either the first or last survey, using IndVal.g with 999 permutations. P-values are Benjamini–Hochberg FDR-corrected.

group_vec <- ifelse(meta_strict$sample == "first", 1L, 2L)

indval_result <- multipatt(
  mat_strict,
  cluster = group_vec,
  control = how(nperm = 999),
  func    = "IndVal.g",
  duleg   = TRUE
)

indval_tbl <- indval_result$sign %>%
  rownames_to_column("taxon") %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  rename(group = index) %>%
  mutate(
    survey = if_else(group == 1L, "first", "last"),
    p_adj  = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH")
  ) %>%
  filter(p_adj <= 0.05) %>%
  arrange(p_adj)

0 taxa pass FDR ≤ 5%: 0 associated with the first survey, 0 with the last survey.

4.1 Full indicator taxa table

print_full(
  indval_tbl %>%
    select(taxon, survey, stat, p.value, p_adj) %>%
    mutate(sig = case_when(p_adj < 0.001 ~ "***",
                           p_adj < 0.01  ~ "**",
                           p_adj < 0.05  ~ "*",
                           TRUE          ~ "ns")),
  caption = "Indicator taxa (IndVal.g, FDR ≤ 5%). 'first' = associated with first survey; 'last' = last survey."
)
Indicator taxa (IndVal.g, FDR ≤ 5%). ‘first’ = associated with first survey; ‘last’ = last survey.
taxon survey stat p.value p_adj sig

4.2 Top 15 per survey — bar chart

top_indval <- indval_tbl %>%
  group_by(survey) %>%
  slice_min(order_by = p_adj, n = 15, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

if (nrow(top_indval) > 0 && length(unique(top_indval$survey)) > 0) {
  ggplot(top_indval,
         aes(x = stat, y = reorder(taxon, stat), fill = survey)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
    facet_wrap(
      ~survey,
      scales  = "free_y",
      labeller = as_labeller(c(first = "First survey", last = "Last survey"))
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(first = "#2980b9", last = "#e67e22")) +
    labs(
      title    = "Indicator taxa — first vs last survey",
      subtitle = "IndVal.g statistic; top 15 per group (FDR ≤ 5%)",
      x = "IndVal statistic", y = NULL, fill = NULL
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA))
} else {
  ggplot() +
    theme_void() +
    ggtitle("No indicator taxa to display (top_indval is empty)")
}

Top 15 indicator taxa per survey by IndVal.g statistic (FDR ≤ 5%).

Part 5 — Univariate change in community descriptors

Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test for each continuous driver variable. Each plot contributes one matched pair (first survey value, last survey value).

univar_vars <- intersect(
  c("háplöntuþekja", "vegetation_height_mean", "total_cover", "soil_depth"),
  names(meta_strict)
)

env_paired_wide <- meta_strict %>%
  select(plot_number, sample, all_of(univar_vars)) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = sample,
    values_from = all_of(univar_vars),
    names_sep   = "_"
  )

wilcox_results <- map_dfr(univar_vars, function(v) {
  first_col    <- paste0(v, "_first")
  last_col     <- paste0(v, "_last")
  x            <- env_paired_wide[[first_col]]
  y            <- env_paired_wide[[last_col]]
  complete_idx <- complete.cases(x, y)
  n_pairs      <- sum(complete_idx)
  test <- wilcox.test(y[complete_idx], x[complete_idx],
                      paired = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE)
  tibble(
    variable      = v,
    n_pairs       = n_pairs,
    median_first  = median(x[complete_idx], na.rm = TRUE),
    median_last   = median(y[complete_idx], na.rm = TRUE),
    median_change = median(y[complete_idx] - x[complete_idx], na.rm = TRUE),
    ci_low        = test$conf.int[1],
    ci_high       = test$conf.int[2],
    W             = test$statistic,
    p_value       = test$p.value
  )
}) %>%
  mutate(
    p_adj = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH"),
    sig   = case_when(p_adj < 0.001 ~ "***",
                      p_adj < 0.01  ~ "**",
                      p_adj < 0.05  ~ "*",
                      TRUE          ~ "ns")
  )

5.1 Results table

print_full(wilcox_results,
           caption = "Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test results. median_change = last − first. BH-adjusted p-values.")
Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test results. median_change = last − first. BH-adjusted p-values.
variable n_pairs median_first median_last median_change ci_low ci_high W p_value p_adj sig
háplöntuþekja 160 56.7731 73.3881 6.8194 7.5554 12.0418 8977 0.0000 0.0000 ***
vegetation_height_mean 160 10.6492 10.6125 -0.2074 -0.5230 1.2701 6821 0.4284 0.4284 ns
total_cover 160 98.2562 98.4293 0.0000 -0.3383 2.6627 4250 0.1482 0.1976 ns
soil_depth 160 102.5879 104.5349 -0.3077 -8.6477 -1.3871 2652 0.0072 0.0145 *

5.2 Change distributions

env_paired_long_change <- map_dfr(univar_vars, function(v) {
  first_col <- paste0(v, "_first")
  last_col  <- paste0(v, "_last")
  env_paired_wide %>%
    transmute(
      plot_number = plot_number,
      variable    = v,
      change      = .data[[last_col]] - .data[[first_col]]
    )
}) %>%
  filter(!is.na(change)) %>%
  left_join(wilcox_results %>% select(variable, p_adj, sig), by = "variable")

y_top <- max(env_paired_long_change$change, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.07

ggplot(env_paired_long_change, aes(x = variable, y = change)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_violin(fill = "#bdc3c7", alpha = 0.5, trim = TRUE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.18, fill = "white", outlier.size = 0.8) +
  geom_text(
    data = env_paired_long_change %>% distinct(variable, sig),
    aes(y = y_top, label = sig),
    size = 5, colour = "#c0392b"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Change in continuous community descriptors (last − first)",
    subtitle = "Paired Wilcoxon; * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001 (BH-adjusted)",
    x = NULL, y = "Change (last − first)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11)

Distribution of per-plot change (last − first) for each community descriptor. Significance stars are BH-adjusted.

Part 6 — Excluded coastal plots

With only 6 rows (3 plots × 2 surveys), NMDS is numerically unstable. PCoA (metric multidimensional scaling) is used instead.

outlier_rows <- meta_strict$plot_number %in% outlier_plots
mat_coastal  <- mat_strict[outlier_rows, ]
meta_coastal <- meta_strict[outlier_rows, ]
mat_coastal  <- mat_coastal[, colSums(mat_coastal) > 0]

pcoa_coastal <- cmdscale(
  vegdist(mat_coastal, method = "bray"),
  k   = 2,
  eig = TRUE
)

pcoa_df <- as.data.frame(pcoa_coastal$points) %>%
  setNames(c("Axis1", "Axis2")) %>%
  bind_cols(meta_coastal %>% select(plot_number, sample))

var_explained <- round(
  100 * pcoa_coastal$eig[1:2] / sum(abs(pcoa_coastal$eig)), 1
)

ggplot(pcoa_df, aes(x = Axis1, y = Axis2)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = plot_number), colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = sample, shape = sample), size = 3) +
  geom_label_repel(
    aes(label = paste(plot_number, sample)),
    size = 3, max.overlaps = 20, seed = 1
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(first = "#2980b9", last = "#e67e22"),
                      labels = c(first = "First survey", last = "Last survey")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values  = c(first = 16, last = 17),
                     labels  = c(first = "First survey", last = "Last survey")) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title    = "PCoA — excluded coastal/outlier plots",
    subtitle = sprintf("Bray–Curtis · Axis 1 = %.1f%% · Axis 2 = %.1f%%",
                       var_explained[1], var_explained[2]),
    x = sprintf("PCoA Axis 1 (%.1f%%)", var_explained[1]),
    y = sprintf("PCoA Axis 2 (%.1f%%)", var_explained[2]),
    colour = NULL, shape = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

PCoA of the three excluded coastal/outlier plots. Labels show plot name and survey. Lines connect first and last survey of the same plot. 

# Top species in each coastal plot (last survey only, for brevity)
coastal_last <- meta_coastal %>%
  filter(sample == "last") %>%
  pull(plot_number)

coastal_species <- community_long_paired %>%
  filter(plot_number %in% coastal_last, sample == "last") %>%
  arrange(plot_number, desc(cover)) %>%
  group_by(plot_number) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  ungroup()

print_full(coastal_species,
           caption = "Top 10 species by cover in each coastal plot (last survey).")
Top 10 species by cover in each coastal plot (last survey).
plot_number sample taxon_name cover
RN-02 last Leymus arenarius 1.9000
RN-03 last Honckenya peploides 38.6667
RN-03 last Mertensia maritima 24.4000
RN-03 last Leymus arenarius 16.8333
RN-03 last Cakile maritima 0.8000
RN-65-01 last Puccinellia coarctata 58.8333
RN-65-01 last Plantago maritima 14.3833

Part 7 — Suggested next steps

Based on the results above, the following analyses are the logical continuation — choose based on what the figures showed.

A — dbRDA (distance-based redundancy analysis) If háplöntuþekja or vegetation height changed significantly in Part 5, use dbRDA to formally partition variance between the temporal effect (sample) and the environmental gradient, while conditioning on plot identity:

vegan::dbrda(
  mat_strict ~ sample + háplöntuþekja + Condition(plot_number),
  data   = meta_strict,
  method = "bray"
)

B — Stratum-specific PERMANOVAs If soil type or moisture was significant in the Kruskal–Wallis (Part 3), split the dataset by those classes and re-run paired PERMANOVA within each stratum — this tests whether the temporal signal is consistent across habitat types or concentrated in one.

C — Taxon-level ecology Review the indicator taxa from Part 4 against Icelandic vegetation literature. Key questions: - Are the ‘last survey’ gainers typically associated with warming, reduced grazing, or nitrogen deposition in Icelandic heathlands? - Do Racomitrium or Dryas appear as consistent losers? - Are wetland sedges (Carex lyngbyei, C. nigra) showing directional change?

D — Spatial autocorrelation of turnover Test whether compositional change (the per-plot Bray–Curtis from Part 3) is spatially structured using a Mantel test:

geo_dist <- dist(cbind(env_first$longitude_1, env_first$latitude_1))
vegan::mantel(bc_dist_matrix, geo_dist, method = "pearson", permutations = 999)
